title: 8 Herbal Creams For Chapped Lips
intro_desc: Herbal creams for chapped lips are made with natural ingredients that help soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. One reason herbal teas relieve chapped lips is because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. For example, Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, has been used for centuries to treat skin irritations due to its cooling and moisturizing effects. When applied to chapped lips, aloe vera helps lock in moisture and promote skin cell regeneration. Another herb, Calendula officinalis, or marigold, has antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. Symphytum officinalis, or comfrey, contains allantoin, a compound that stimulates collagen production and helps repair damaged skin tissue. These herbs work together to create a barrier that protects chapped lips from further irritation and promotes healthy skin growth. When used regularly, herbal creams can provide long-lasting relief from chapped lips and help prevent future dryness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains aloin, barbaloin, and cinnamic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to calm and protect chapped lips. Aloin and barbaloin work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing, while cinnamic acid has antiseptic properties that prevent infection and promote tissue repair. The gel from the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which help to lock in moisture and protect the lips from environmental stressors. By applying aloe vera cream to chapped lips, you can help to repair and restore their natural moisture and texture.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves.
Combine 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax mixture over low heat until melted.
Mix 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of honey with the melted coconut oil mixture.
Add the Aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well. Pour into a container and let cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are active constituents that contribute to its healing properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them effective in soothing and protecting chapped lips. The triterpenoids in Calendula officinalis creams help to reduce redness and inflammation, while the flavonoids and carotenoids promote tissue repair and regeneration. The antimicrobial properties of Calendula officinalis creams also help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for skin repair. By applying Calendula officinalis creams to chapped lips, you can take advantage of these properties to help heal and protect your skin.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat. Add the beeswax and stir until melted.
Add the Calendula officinalis flowers to the saucepan and steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Combine the strained mixture with shea butter and vitamin E oil. Mix well until smooth.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Your Calendula officinalis cream for chapped lips is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains allantoin, a boiactive constituent that helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. It also contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that forms a barrier on the skin's surface, preventing dry air from drying out the lips further. Allantoin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, while mucilage helps to repair and regenerate damaged skin cells. The combination of these properties makes Symphytum officinale creams effective in providing long-lasting hydration and relief for chapped lips. By creating a protective barrier and promoting skin repair, Symphytum officinale creams can help to heal and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Symphytum officinale tincture, and a few drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and add Symphytum officinale tincture and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and solidify before using as a lip cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents limonene, menthone, and menthol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling on chapped lips. Menthone also has a soothing effect, calming the skin and easing irritation. Menthol, on the other hand, helps lock in moisture and protect the skin from dry air. By applying Mentha x piperita creams, you can provide your chapped lips with the necessary hydration and protection they need to heal and feel better.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of Mentha x piperita essential oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in Mentha x piperita essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a lip balm tin or a small container and let it cool and harden completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and irritation. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that calm the skin and reduce redness, while flavonoids help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy skin cell growth. The soothing and moisturizing effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help to repair and restore the skin's natural barrier, leaving lips feeling smooth and soft. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to prevent dryness and chapping, keeping lips healthy and hydrated.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts.
Add 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the saucepan.
Add 10 drops of licorice extract (Glycyrrhiza glabra) and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the saucepan.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool until it hardens. Use the cream as needed to moisturize chapped lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains saponins, flavonoids, and volatile oils that help soothe and protect dry, chapped lips. The saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling, while the flavonoids have antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage. The volatile oils, including borneol and borneol acetate, have antibacterial properties, preventing infection and promoting healing. These compounds work together to moisturize and repair dry skin, making Urtica dioica creams a popular natural remedy for chapped lips. Urtica dioica creams can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick relief and promoting healthy lip skin.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1/2 cup of Urtica dioica leaves, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Combine Urtica dioica leaves and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the leaves.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and shea butter over low heat. Stir until combined.
Add the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to the Urtica dioica mixture. Stir well and pour into lip balm containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm chapped lips by reducing redness and swelling. The antioxidant properties of melissa officinalis also help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage, promoting healthy skin cell growth. Additionally, melissa officinalis creams often contain antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing in chapped lips. The combination of these properties makes melissa officinalis creams a popular choice for soothing and moisturizing chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis essential oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the Melissa officinalis essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers or lip balm tubes. Let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, your Melissa officinalis creams for chapped lips are ready to use. Apply as needed to soothe and moisturize dry lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids. These constituents work together to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect chapped lips. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with chapped lips. The glycoproteins in the cream help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and healthy. The phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea creams also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy lip tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for chapped lips
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of honey, and 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add honey and dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Pour into lip balm tubes or containers.
Let the mixture cool and harden completely. Store in a cool, dry place and use as needed to soothe chapped lips.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chapped lips. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have soothing properties that can calm dry skin. These teas can also help keep your skin hydrated from the inside out. Regularly drinking these teas might keep your lips smooth and healthy, but it depends on your individual skin needs.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for chapped lips are generally considered safe to consume daily, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some teas, like peppermint or chamomile, can be soothing, but others, like licorice root, might cause issues if consumed excessively. Be mindful of your body's reaction and adjust your intake accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chapped lips, but it takes some time. Results are usually seen within 1-3 weeks. Some people notice improvement in 1 week, while others may take 3. This is because the skin on your lips has to heal and regenerate. Drinking herbal tea regularly can help speed up the process.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chapped lips can be most effective in the morning, before the day's activities. This allows the tea's healing properties to soak into your skin, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort. Its benefits will last throughout the day, helping to keep your lips soft and moisturized.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for chapped lips. For example, if you're taking a medication that thins your blood, drinking certain teas like ginkgo biloba or feverfew can increase the risk of bleeding. This can make chapped lips worse or harder to heal.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's generally a good idea to check the ingredients of herbal teas for chapped lips. Some herbs like peppermint and chamomile are usually safe, but others like sage and parsley might not be. It's also essential to check the caffeine content and make sure the tea is free of any contaminants.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing relief for chapped lips, but some ingredients might not be safe for breastfeeding. For example, sage and peppermint can affect milk supply and might not be good for your baby. If you're breastfeeding and want to try herbal teas, choose ones that are known to be safe, like chamomile or calendula.
title_2: 8 Natural Herbal Creams for Outdoor Workers to Cure Chapped Lips
